ActionItems: Lock a Hoboken or Jersey City lease at or below 3600 a month before signing
Hold food cost to 30 percent by sourcing seconds and whole animals from Hudson Valley
Need 58 covers a night at a 58 dollar average check to clear break-even five nights a week
Add a Saturday brunch service to push past break-even into reserve building
Revisit payroll once the line is staffed at three cooks plus two front-of-house
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: First full projection for Rootstock, a 40-seat seasonal-American room in Jersey City. Modeling a five-night dinner service plus weekend brunch to find the cover count where the room pays for itself.

CurrentState.Note: Schema keys inherited from the QuickBooks break-even canonical template (originally modeled for a fitness studio). Persona mapping for Rootstock (restaurant): AaronDrawFromNet = Cynthia's owner draw target; InstructorPay_Raj = full kitchen + front-of-house payroll line; MembershipDues represents booked dinner covers (Members = monthly covers, Rate = per-cover contribution, Total = monthly contribution); DropIns represents walk-in and bar-only seating; EquipmentSales represents retail bread and pantry preserves. BreakEvenMembers in the scenarios represents the break-even dinner-cover count per month.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: The room breaks even at roughly 52 covers a night. Everything above 58 covers is what funds the reserve and pays me. Food cost is the lever that moves break-even faster than rent does.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: At a 3600 rent and 30 percent food cost, fixed costs divide by a 40 dollar contribution per cover to land break-even near 52 covers a night.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Comfortable. Five dinner nights at 58 covers clears costs and leaves room for the reserve.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Base lease at 3600 a month

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Tighter. A 600 dollar rent bump pushes break-even to about 55 covers a night and trims the reserve unless brunch picks up the slack.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Lease climbs to 4200 a month
